source: VendorGuard AI — Benchmark Evidence Ledger. Three hand-written sample DPAs used to demonstrate Layer 5 Contract Intelligence against strong, ambiguous, and weak contract language. Text is original; patterned after common MSA / DPA boilerplate.

dpas.label: Strong DPA (India-resident, explicit 24h breach SLA, audit rights)
dpas.text:
DATA PROCESSING ADDENDUM — DataFort India Pvt Ltd ("Processor") and Customer ("Controller").

1. Purpose limitation. Processor shall Process Personal Data only for the documented purposes of the Services and only on documented instructions of Controller. Purpose limitation is strictly enforced; no use of Personal Data for any purpose outside this Addendum is permitted. A written contract governs all Sub-processor relationships.

2. Security safeguards. Processor shall implement reasonable security safeguards including encryption at rest (AES-256), encryption in transit (TLS 1.2+), multi-factor authentication for privileged access, strict least-privilege IAM, centralised access control, continuous vulnerability management and 24x7 SOC monitoring. Audit logs retained for 12 months in accordance with Rule 6(e) of the DPDP Rules, 2025.

3. Breach notification. Processor shall notify Controller within 24 hours of becoming aware of any personal data breach, providing (a) description, (b) timing, (c) consequences, (d) mitigation measures and (e) a designated contact person. Controller's own 72-hour obligation to the Data Protection Board is explicitly acknowledged.

4. Data residency. All personal data shall reside in India. Data will reside in India Region only. No cross-border transfer outside India without Controller's prior written consent and only to jurisdictions notified by the Central Government under §16 of the DPDP Act, 2023.

5. Retention & erasure. Data will be deleted within 30 days of termination. Retention period is strictly limited to the documented purpose. Certificate of deletion issued on request, per §8(7) of the DPDP Act, 2023.

6. Data Principal rights. Processor shall assist Controller in responding to Data Principal rights requests (access, correction, erasure, nomination) within 14 days at no additional cost, in accordance with Chapter III of the DPDP Act, 2023.

7. Grievance redressal. A designated grievance officer is appointed; grievance mechanism is published on the Processor portal and complaints are acknowledged within 7 working days.

8. Consent and lawful basis. Processor will only Process Personal Data where Controller has obtained free, specific, informed, unconditional and unambiguous consent of the data principal (or where a Legitimate Use under §7 applies).

9. Audit. Processor grants audit rights to Controller, including annual on-site inspection and independent SOC 2 / ISO 27001 reports on request.

10. DPO and DPIA. Processor cooperates with Controller's Data Protection Officer and contributes to any Data Protection Impact Assessment required for a Significant Data Fiduciary.

11. Children's data. Processor shall not Process personal data of a child without verifiable parental consent in accordance with Rule 10 of the DPDP Rules, 2025; no behavioural tracking of children; no targeted advertising to children.

12. Privacy notice. A plain-language privacy notice is made available in English and the scheduled languages required under §5 of the DPDP Act, 2023.

dpas.label: Ambiguous DPA (vague SLAs, silent on children/audit/residency)
dpas.text:
DATA PROCESSING ADDENDUM — TechPartner India LLP ("Vendor").

1. Services. Vendor will process customer data to deliver the Services described in the Statement of Work. Vendor acts as a data processor on behalf of Customer.

2. Security. Vendor will use industry-standard security measures to protect customer data, including encryption at rest and in transit, and access control. Vendor maintains periodic logging of security events.

3. Breach handling. In the event of a security incident that Vendor becomes aware of, Vendor will notify Customer without undue delay and cooperate in any subsequent investigation.

4. Data locations. Vendor operates globally and customer data may be processed in any jurisdiction where Vendor or its sub-processors maintain infrastructure, subject to applicable laws.

5. Retention. Vendor will retain customer data during the term of the Agreement. Retention beyond termination may apply for backup and legal reasons.

6. Rights of individuals. Vendor will reasonably assist Customer with requests from individuals exercising their rights under applicable privacy laws, to the extent such assistance is technically feasible.

7. Termination. On termination, Vendor will delete or return customer data upon Customer's written request within a commercially reasonable timeframe.

8. Sub-processors. Vendor may engage sub-processors to support the Services. A list of current sub-processors is available on request.

dpas.label: Weak DPA (US data centres, best-efforts security, indefinite retention, no audit)
dpas.text:
VENDOR SERVICES AGREEMENT — DataBridge Inc.

1. Scope. Vendor provides services to Customer. Vendor may use customer data for any purpose related to service improvement, analytics, research, and marketing. No restriction on use beyond applicable law.

2. Security. Vendor will use best efforts to secure data. Vendor's platform is provided as-is and as-available with no warranty of security. Logs retained for 30 days.

3. Data storage. Vendor stores data in the United States data center, and may store data in any jurisdiction where Vendor or its sub-processors maintain infrastructure.

4. Breach. Vendor will inform Customer as soon as practicable after becoming aware of a material security incident affecting production systems.

5. Retention. Vendor retains data indefinitely unless Customer specifically requests deletion in writing, subject to a processing fee. No deletion is performed automatically.

6. Audit. No audit rights are granted under this Agreement. Vendor may share SOC 2 Type I summaries subject to NDA.

7. Individuals' rights. Vendor does not support data subject requests directly; any such requests must be handled by Customer at additional cost to Controller.

8. Children. Vendor may track, profile, and target users under 18 with personalised ads for children as part of standard ad-tech personalisation. No age verification is performed.

9. Consent. Consent is deemed to be given by continued use of the Services; opt-out only.

10. Exemptions. Vendor relies on a blanket exemption: the DPDP does not apply to this processing because it is performed for research and statistical purposes only.
